---
title: "Shih-Feng (Fredric) Huang, MD"
subtitle: "Robotic Colorectal Surgical Intelligence: planning, proficiency, and outcomes"
---

Attending Colorectal Surgeon at Kaohsiung Veterans General Hospital. Building a unified framework that links preoperative planning, intraoperative proficiency, and postoperative outcomes in robotic colorectal surgery.

Concurrently a PhD candidate in Computer Science (Medical Informatics track) at National Cheng Kung University.

## Selected recent work

   - A fully automated CT-based pelvimetry pipeline for quantifying mid-pelvic surgical workspace in rectal cancer. *Int J CARS*. 2026. [doi](https://doi.org/10.1007/s11548-026-03606-2)
   - Technical proficiency assessment of robotic intracorporeal single-stapling colorectal anastomosis using video-based RA-CUSUM. *Int J Colorectal Dis*. 2026. [doi](https://doi.org/10.1007/s00384-025-05078-3)
   - Machine learning–based risk modeling for safety-focused learning curve assessment in robotic left-sided colorectal cancer surgery. *J Robotic Surg*. 2026. [doi](https://doi.org/10.1007/s11701-025-03088-5)

See [Research](research.qmd) for the full framework, or [Publications](publications.qmd) for the complete list.

→ Flagship white paper coming June 2026.
